title: Best VPN for Android in 2026: Top Picks for Privacy, Streaming, and Speed
description: The best VPN apps for Android in 2026: which ones offer the fastest speeds, the best privacy, and work reliably for streaming Netflix and other services on mobile.
content:
Android is the world's most used mobile operating system, and most Android users carry their phones on public Wi-Fi networks -- coffee shops, airports, gyms -- daily. A VPN on your Android phone protects your traffic on those networks and lets you access streaming content from other regions. Here is what to pick in 2026.

What to Look for in an Android VPN App

The Android app quality matters as much as the underlying VPN service. A good Android VPN app: connects automatically when you join an untrusted network (auto-connect on Wi-Fi), has a kill switch that blocks internet if the VPN drops (preventing accidental unencrypted traffic), supports WireGuard or a WireGuard-based protocol for fast speeds and low battery drain, and has a split tunneling option so you can route only certain apps through the VPN. The last point matters on Android because some banking apps and local services block VPN traffic. Split tunneling lets you exclude those apps while routing everything else through the VPN.

NordVPN for Android: Best Overall

NordVPN's Android app is the most polished among major VPN providers. It uses NordLynx (NordVPN's WireGuard implementation) by default, which is the fastest protocol currently available. The app auto-connects on untrusted networks, has a reliable kill switch, and includes Threat Protection Lite (blocking ads and malware at the DNS level without a separate app). NordVPN's no-logs policy has been independently audited multiple times. Streaming performance is strong -- it reliably unblocks Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and most other services.

Mullvad for Android: Best for Privacy

Mullvad is the most privacy-focused option. You create an account with a randomly generated account number (no email, no personal data). You pay with cash, cryptocurrency, or card, but the payment is not linked to your usage data. The Android app is open source and has been audited by security researchers. Mullvad does not keep logs and has been raided by Swedish police -- who found nothing because there was nothing to find. If privacy is your primary concern rather than streaming or features, Mullvad is the correct choice. It does not have a dedicated streaming-optimization layer, so Netflix unblocking is less consistent.

Surfshark for Android: Best Value

Surfshark allows unlimited simultaneous connections on one subscription -- useful if you have multiple Android devices or want to cover family members. The Android app is clean and supports WireGuard. Performance is slightly below NordVPN but the gap has narrowed significantly in 2025. Long-term pricing (2-year plans) makes Surfshark among the cheapest credible options.

Free Options: Proton VPN Only

If you need a free VPN on Android, Proton VPN's free tier is the only trustworthy option. No data selling, no bandwidth cap (speeds are limited), and servers in Switzerland, the Netherlands, and the US. For light privacy use on public Wi-Fi, it works. For streaming or consistent high-speed use, the paid tier is necessary. Avoid all other free VPNs on the Google Play Store -- the vast majority monetize by selling your data, which is the opposite of what a VPN is for.

Setup Tips for Android

After installing your chosen VPN: enable auto-connect on untrusted Wi-Fi in settings. Enable the kill switch. Set your preferred protocol to WireGuard (or NordLynx/Lightway for NordVPN and ExpressVPN). Configure split tunneling to exclude your banking app, local delivery apps, or anything that blocks VPN traffic. Most VPN apps detect when a network is untrusted automatically -- you can set them to connect without any manual action each time you join a new network.
